title: podman system service times out even with active connections
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Launching `podman --log-level=debug system service -t60` times out after 60 seconds even when connections are still in use

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman --log-level=debug system service -t60

2. launch a long connection via the API (in my case, using giltab-runner)

3. what podman close it self while the container should still be running

**Describe the results you received:**
DEBU[0012] Started container 1fa59ca9be2274238a4b3f5b8f115ba12451da6aa984bc3c58619679b639604f 
DEBU[0012] IdleTracker 0x4000118020:idle 4/48 connection(s) 
DEBU[0012] STDIN copy completed                         
DEBU[0021] IdleTracker 0x4000012560:closed 3/48 connection(s) 
DEBU[0022] IdleTracker 0x4000118020:closed 3/48 connection(s) 
DEBU[0082] API Server idle for 1m0s                     
DEBU[0082] APIServer.Shutdown by pkg/api/server/server.go:184, 3/48 connection(s) 
DEBU[0082] APIServer.Shutdown by cmd/podman/system/service_abi.go:52, 3/48 connection(s) 
DEBU[0142] Called service.PersistentPostRunE(podman --log-level=debug system service -t60) 


**Describe the results you expected:**
podman doesn't close while it is servicing connections

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.1.2-dev

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: arm64
  buildahVersion: 1.16.1
  cgroupManager: systemd

  cpus: 4

    version: "32"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: f32-podman
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.8.11-200.fc32.aarch64

  memFree: 1086005248
  memTotal: 4099502080
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.15-2.fc32.aarch64

    version: |-
      crun version 0.15

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux

  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:

  swapFree: 1953210368

    number: 3
    paused: 0

    stopped: 3
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 5
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
aarch64 host (CentOS8) running aarch64 vm (Fedora 32)
same thing can be replicated using x86_64 laptop
